query: Attention Is All You Need Vaswani et al. arXiv year
answer: "Attention Is All You Need" by Vaswani et al. was published in 2017 on arXiv. The paper introduced the Transformer model, which uses attention mechanisms instead of recurrence and convolutions.

results.title: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., ArXiv 2017) | Jonathan K. Kummerfeld
results.snippet: To get context-dependence without recurrence we can use a network that applies attention multiple times over both input and output (as it is generated). The idea in this work is to use attention, applied multiple times, to get a network that is fast while still capturing positional information. * *Positional encoding*, which is a vector of the same length as the word representation, but that depends only on the position in the input. The more general form of this described in the paper has keys ($K$), queries ($Q$) and values ($V$), but the network uses the same vector for the key and value. Note, on the left hand side attention is over all input words, while on the right it is only over the words produced so far (ie. * Other details, (1) When the outputs are words, the vectors used to initially represent each input word are also used to represent the outputs and in the final linear transformation (though with some rescaling).

results.title: [PDF] Attention is All you Need | Semantic Scholar
results.snippet: A new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely is proposed, which generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.Expand. A new architecture to proficiently mines the ability of attention mechanism and stacked recurrent neural networks for neural machine translation (NMT) tasks is proposed.Expand. This paper's simplified architecture consists in the decoder part of a transformer model, based on self-attention, but with locality constraints applied on the attention receptive field, which achieves a new state of the art of 35.7 BLEU on IWSLT'14 German-English.Expand. This paper illustrates the approach to the shared task on similar language translation in the fifth conference on machine translation (WMT-20) with a recurrence based layered encoder-decoder model with the Transformer model that enjoys the benefits of both Recurrent Attention and Transformer.Expand.

results.title: Attention is all you need - ACM Digital Library
results.snippet: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.
